nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.notes:
Accepts POST with JSON body: { label1_base64, label2_base64, sku }
Both PDF files must be base64-encoded strings.

nodes.name: Extract Label 1
nodes.notes:
Calls Claude API to extract all fields from label 1.
Credential: create an HTTP Header Auth credential named 'Anthropic API'
with header name 'x-api-key' and your Anthropic API key as the value.

nodes.name: Log to Google Sheets
nodes.notes:
SETUP REQUIRED:
1. Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_HERE with your sheet's ID (from its URL)
2. Create a Google Sheets OAuth2 credential in n8n
3. Make sure the sheet has a tab named 'Change Log' with these column headers:
   Timestamp | SKU | Changes Found | Total Fields | Status | Differences

nodes.name: Notify Slack
nodes.notes:
SETUP REQUIRED:
1. Replace YOUR_SLACK_CHANNEL_ID with your compliance/design channel ID
   (right-click the channel in Slack → View channel details → copy the ID at the bottom)
2. Create a Slack OAuth2 credential in n8n

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ sku: $('Compare Labels').first().json.sku, total_differences: $('Compare Labels').first().json.total_differences, total_matches: $('Compare Labels').first().json.total_matches, status: $('Compare Labels').first().json.total_differences > 0 ? 'CHANGES' : 'MATCH', differences: $('Compare Labels').first().json.differences }) }}

setup_steps: 1. Import this file into n8n (Workflows → Import from file)
2. In the 'Log to Google Sheets' node: replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID_HERE with your sheet's ID
3. In the 'Notify Slack' node: replace YOUR_SLACK_CHANNEL_ID with your channel's ID
4. Create credentials in n8n: Anthropic HTTP Header Auth, Google Sheets OAuth2, Slack OAuth2
5. Activate the workflow — the webhook URL appears in the Webhook node
6. Trigger it by POSTing JSON: { label1_base64: '...', label2_base64: '...', sku: 'SKU-001' }
__README__.anthropic_credential: HTTP Header Auth — header name: x-api-key, value: your Anthropic API key
__README__.encoding_pdfs: To base64-encode a PDF before sending: python -c "import base64; print(base64.b64encode(open('label.pdf','rb').read()).decode())"
